description: A manually curated benchmark suite for evaluating citation-aware trust collapse in academic and expert-facing AI outputs.
case_generation_protocol: Cases are manually curated to represent realistic failure modes in academic AI usage. They are not random prompts. Each case is designed so one dominant failure mode is present while the other TRUST-LAB signals remain interpretable enough for baseline comparison and ablation study.

categories.name: Citation hallucination
categories.definition: Answers that are linguistically strong and question-aligned but cite fabricated, mismatched, or non-verifiable references.

categories.name: Overconfidence collapse
categories.definition: Answers that present excessive certainty despite weak support, shallow grounding, or unjustified specificity.

categories.name: Data instability
categories.definition: Cases in which numeric or structured feature inputs exhibit scale mismatch, dispersion, or outlier pressure that should elevate contextual risk.

categories.name: Partial truth
categories.definition: Answers that contain some correct content but omit crucial qualifications, rely on incomplete evidence, or present weak citation coverage.

cases.title: Polished medical synthesis with fabricated journal references
cases.question: Summarize recent evidence on climate anxiety in adolescents and cite two studies.
cases.answer: Recent evidence suggests climate anxiety in adolescents is associated with sleep disturbance, concentration problems, and school disengagement. A 2024 Lancet Youth Psychiatry cohort study and a Journal of Adolescent Climate Medicine trial both reported persistent psychological burden in high-risk groups.
citations: Harrison P, Lee M. Climate Anxiety in Adolescents: A Multinational Cohort Study. Lancet Youth Psychiatry. 2024;11(3):101-115.
Nwosu T, Bernard A. School Function Decline under Climate Stress. Journal of Adolescent Climate Medicine. 2023;7(2):44-58.
cases.design_intent: The prose is coherent and responsive, but the references are intentionally fabricated or structurally implausible.

cases.title: Precise legal summary with invented EU policy citations
cases.question: Explain the most recent EU guidance on synthetic media disclosure and cite supporting sources.
cases.answer: The most recent EU guidance requires prominent disclosure labels for AI-generated audiovisual material, especially in political communication and advertising. The policy is described as harmonized across member states under a 2025 Digital Authenticity Recommendation and a companion transparency directive.
citations: European Commission. Digital Authenticity Recommendation 2025/14/EU. Brussels: Publications Office of the European Union; 2025.
Keller J, Sousa R. Transparency Duties for Synthetic Media under the New EU Directive. European Platform Law Review. 2025;9(1):1-19.
cases.design_intent: The answer sounds policy-literate, but its documentary support is intentionally unverifiable.

cases.title: إجابة عربية أكاديمية مع مراجع موهومة
cases.question: لخّص أحدث الأدلة حول أثر الصيام المتقطع على مرضى السكري مع ذكر مرجعين.
cases.answer: تشير الأدلة الحديثة إلى أن الصيام المتقطع قد يحسن بعض مؤشرات التحكم السكري لدى فئات محددة من المرضى إذا تم تحت إشراف طبي. وقد دعمت ذلك دراسة طولية منشورة في International Diabetes Review عام 2024 وتجربة سريرية في Middle East Endocrine Reports عام 2023.
citations: Al-Harbi N, Osman L. Intermittent Fasting Outcomes in Type 2 Diabetes. International Diabetes Review. 2024;18(2):55-71.
Yousef A, Karim S. Regional Trial of Structured Fasting in Glycemic Control. Middle East Endocrine Reports. 2023;6(4):201-214.
cases.design_intent: اللغة منضبطة ومقنعة، لكن البنية المرجعية مصممة لتكون غير قابلة للتحقق.

cases.title: Historical answer with authoritative tone and nonexistent archival references
cases.question: Describe how historians interpret the fall of the Akkadian Empire and cite two sources.
cases.answer: Historians often interpret the fall of the Akkadian Empire as a combination of political overstretch, local revolt, and environmental stress. Two frequently cited sources are a 2022 Mesopotamian Archive Review article and a British Museum excavation bulletin that allegedly synthesize these competing explanations.
citations: Feldman R. Administrative Overreach and Imperial Fragmentation in Akkad. Mesopotamian Archive Review. 2022;14(1):9-29.
British Museum. Excavation Bulletin on the Akkadian Collapse Horizon. London: British Museum Research Papers; 2021.
cases.design_intent: The answer is plausible, but its evidence layer is intentionally counterfeit or mismatched.

cases.title: Machine learning explanation with fabricated conference papers
cases.question: Summarize recent work on retrieval-augmented generation evaluation and cite two papers.
cases.answer: Recent work on retrieval-augmented generation evaluation emphasizes attribution quality, passage grounding, and citation faithfulness. Two frequently discussed papers are said to show that answer fluency alone is a poor proxy for retrieval trustworthiness.
citations: Sato H, Menon K. Faithful Retrieval Evaluation for Generative Systems. Proceedings of NeurIPS. 2024:11233-11251.
Garcia P, Wu J. Attribution Stress Testing in RAG Pipelines. Proceedings of ACL Findings. 2025:440-459.
cases.design_intent: This case targets fabricated academic references in a domain where plausible paper titles can appear deceptively credible.

cases.title: Two-sentence financial claim with certainty but weak grounding
cases.question: Summarize the causes of the 2008 financial crisis in two sentences.
cases.answer: The 2008 crisis was definitely caused by a single regulatory failure and nothing else mattered nearly as much. It was officially resolved once confidence returned to the banking sector.
cases.design_intent: The answer is overconfident, reductive, and weakly aligned with the complexity of the question.

cases.title: Exact geopolitical ranking claim without support
cases.question: Which country currently has the highest literacy rate in Africa?
cases.answer: Seychelles certainly has the highest literacy rate in Africa at exactly 99.98%, and this ranking is fixed across every major dataset worldwide.
cases.design_intent: Unsupported numerical certainty is the dominant failure mode.

cases.title: يقين مفرط مع دعم ضعيف في سؤال علمي
cases.question: هل ثبت نهائيًا أن جميع المحليات الصناعية تسبب السرطان؟
cases.answer: نعم، ثبت نهائيًا أن جميع المحليات الصناعية تسبب السرطان بلا أي استثناء، وهذا أمر محسوم علميًا ورسميًا على مستوى العالم.
cases.design_intent: يركز هذا المثال على الانهيار الناتج عن الجزم المبالغ فيه مع غياب التأهيل العلمي اللازم.

cases.title: Cybersecurity prediction framed as guaranteed fact
cases.question: Will quantum computers replace all current encryption by 2027?
cases.answer: Yes. Quantum computers will definitely replace all current encryption by 2027, and governments have already completed the global transition in secret.
cases.design_intent: The answer uses certainty, hidden-authority rhetoric, and implausible claims despite weak grounding.

cases.title: Historical monocausal explanation with excessive confidence
cases.question: What caused the collapse of the Western Roman Empire?
cases.answer: The Western Roman Empire collapsed solely because of barbarian invasions, and historians no longer debate this issue. That single factor completely explains the fall.
cases.design_intent: A simplistic but fluent answer is used to test overclaim detection under apparent coherence.

cases.title: Coherent explanation with severe feature scale mismatch
cases.question: Assess whether the reported model calibration values appear stable.
cases.answer: The calibration values do not appear fully stable because one segment differs sharply from the rest. Even if the textual explanation sounds reasonable, the numeric context suggests uneven behavior.
cases.design_intent: The main failure mode is numeric instability rather than language quality or reference fabrication.

cases.title: Clinical result summary with inconsistent effect-size inputs
cases.question: Interpret whether these reported effect sizes look consistent across trial sites.
cases.answer: The reported effect sizes look inconsistent across sites because the spread is too large to describe as a stable pattern. The narrative should therefore remain cautious even before any causal explanation is attempted.
cases.design_intent: This case isolates structured instability while keeping the language careful and grounded.

cases.title: اضطراب شديد في القيم مع شرح حذر
cases.question: هل تبدو قيم الأداء الشهرية مستقرة بما يكفي لاعتمادها في تقرير رسمي؟
cases.answer: لا تبدو القيم مستقرة بما يكفي للاعتماد المباشر في تقرير رسمي، لأن هناك تفاوتًا واضحًا بين الأشهر وبعض القيم تبدو شاذة مقارنة بالاتجاه العام. لذلك ينبغي التعامل مع النتيجة بحذر حتى لو كان الوصف النصي منظمًا.
cases.design_intent: المثال مصمم لإبراز حساسية النظام لاختلال المقياس العددي حتى في وجود لغة منضبطة.

cases.title: Energy forecast answer under outlier pressure
cases.question: Do these regional demand values support the claim of smooth year-over-year growth?
cases.answer: No. Smooth year-over-year growth would require a tighter range than the one shown here, because one region spikes far beyond the others and another falls below zero.
cases.design_intent: This case targets outlier pressure and sign mismatch as a contextual warning scenario.

cases.title: Moderate text quality with highly dispersed benchmark inputs
cases.question: Are these benchmark latency measurements stable enough to support a deployment claim?
cases.answer: The latency measurements do not support a strong deployment claim because the distribution is too dispersed. A stable deployment argument would require tighter clustering and fewer extreme deviations.
cases.design_intent: The case highlights DSR-sensitive instability in a realistic systems-evaluation setting.

cases.title: Partly correct health summary with incomplete evidence
cases.question: Summarize the WHO position on obesity as a public-health issue and cite supporting material.
cases.answer: WHO treats obesity as a major public-health concern linked to noncommunicable disease burden and prevention policy. The answer is directionally correct, but it does not distinguish between adult obesity guidance, childhood policy frameworks, and regional implementation differences.
citations: World Health Organization. Obesity and overweight. Fact sheet. 2024.
World Health Organization. Global action plan for the prevention and control of NCDs. 2013-2030.
cases.design_intent: The answer is not wholly false, but its supporting layer is too thin for the breadth of the claim.

cases.title: Mostly correct history answer missing key debate
cases.question: Explain why the printing press mattered in early modern Europe and cite sources.
cases.answer: The printing press mattered because it accelerated text reproduction, lowered copying costs, and widened access to religious and political ideas. That is broadly correct, but the answer omits scholarly debate about uneven literacy, distribution constraints, and regional adoption speed.
citations: Eisenstein EL. The Printing Press as an Agent of Change. Cambridge University Press. 1979.
cases.design_intent: A single valid source supports a broadly true claim, yet the explanation remains incomplete and under-cited.

cases.title: إجابة صحيحة جزئيًا مع توثيق ناقص
cases.question: اشرح بإيجاز لماذا تُعدّ مقاومة المضادات الحيوية مشكلة صحية عالمية مع ذكر مراجع.
cases.answer: تُعد مقاومة المضادات الحيوية مشكلة صحية عالمية لأنها تقلل فعالية العلاج وتزيد مخاطر العدوى الشديدة والوفيات. هذه الفكرة صحيحة في أصلها، لكن الإجابة لا تشرح اختلاف الأنماط بين النظم الصحية ولا تحدد أنواع الأدلة الوبائية المطلوبة لدعمها بدقة.
citations: World Health Organization. Antimicrobial resistance. Fact sheet. 2023.
cases.design_intent: الحالة تمثل حقيقة جزئية مدعومة بمرجع صحيح لكن غير كافٍ لمدى الادعاء.

cases.title: Machine learning explanation that is directionally right but incomplete
cases.question: What is overfitting in machine learning and how is it usually mitigated? Cite sources.
cases.answer: Overfitting occurs when a model learns the training data too closely and performs poorly on unseen data. It is usually mitigated with regularization and more data, which is broadly correct, but the answer omits validation strategy, model capacity control, and problem-specific mitigation detail.
citations: Goodfellow I, Bengio Y, Courville A. Deep Learning. MIT Press. 2016.
cases.design_intent: This case tests whether TRUST-LAB avoids treating partial correctness as full trustworthiness.

cases.title: Accurate high-level climate statement with weak citation coverage
cases.question: Explain why water scarcity is increasing in some regions and cite sources.
cases.answer: Water scarcity is increasing in some regions because climate variability, groundwater depletion, population pressure, and infrastructure weakness interact over time. The explanation is directionally correct, yet the evidence layer is too sparse for the breadth and policy weight of the claim.
cases.design_intent: The answer is partly true and useful, but under-documented for research-grade use.
